creator: _jkrow
caption:
#ad | @quebeccite is the kind of place where you can ski three different mountains...and still make it back to the city for dinner.

So if you’re craving a winter getaway that doesn’t lock you into one resort all weekend (or you’re traveling with people who want different things), this might be your perfect weekend getaway ⛷️

Here’s exactly how I would spend the perfect winter weekend in @quebeccite:

👉 Save this for your winter getaway wishlist!

Day 1: Arrive in Québec City
- Check into a boutique hotel near Old Québec
- Grab a quick bite and explore the cute cobblestone streets before sunset
- Head straight to Stoneham Mountain Resort for night skiing under the lights (Canada’s largest night skiing terrain and it’s only 25 minutes from downtown)

Day 2:
- Wake up early and head to the slopes at Mont-Sainte-Anne
- In the afternoon, check in to Strøm Spa Nordique (perfect reset after skiing)
- Enjoy a slow dinner in Old Québec

Day 3:
- Head to Le Relais for a casual ski session or lesson (this one is great for beginners or a low key finale to your trip before heading home) OR take an adventure tour in Old Québec
- Before you go, take a quick visit to the Hôtel de Glace (Ice Hotel) to walk through the largest ice hotel in North America

The best part about this itinerary is that everything is so close , so you don’t have to choose between skiing or exploring the historic city, or spend the whole weekend driving.

#bonjourquebec #tourismequebec #quebeccity #montsainteanne #stonehammountain #lerelais #Quebeccite

transcription.text: This is the best place to ski this winter if you love having options. Quebec City is one of the few places where you can ski three different mountains and still make it back to the city for dinner. Here's exactly how I would spend a winter weekend exploring what there is to offer. On day one, arrive by the afternoon and check into your hotel near Old Quebec and then head straight to Stoneham for night skiing under the lights, which is just 25 minutes from downtown. Start day two on the slopes at Mont-Sainte-Anne, the scenic views and a huge range of runs for all levels of skiers. After the morning on the mountain, head back to the city and check into Stromspa Nordique for some ultimate relaxation before grabbing dinner somewhere cozy in the city. On day three, if you're up for more skiing, hit Le Relais for a relaxed final day. It's super close, beginner-friendly, and a great way to wind down a ski weekend in Quebec City. Or skip the slopes altogether and have a fun adventure tour like ice canoeing or biking around the city before heading home. On your way out, don't forget to swing by the famous Ice Hotel. It's the largest in North America and super cool to see in person. Check the caption for the full itinerary.
